audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:Hoe laat + verb + subject + ...?]]. Use it to ask what time an event begins or ends.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces a clock-time wh interrogative with initial [[grammar:hoe laat]], finite-verb-before-subject order, and an event-denoting subject.

audienceSections.normal:
#### Hoe laat ...?

Ask [[grammar:Hoe laat begint de film?]] or [[grammar:Hoe laat eindigt de film?]] to request a clock time.
audienceSections.expert:
#### Hoe laat + verb + subject + ...?

The interrogative phrase occupies first position, finite [[grammar:begint]] or [[grammar:eindigt]] follows, and subject [[grammar:de film]] comes after the verb.

audienceSections.normal: Dutch [[grammar:half negen]] means half past eight: halfway toward nine.
audienceSections.expert: Dutch half-hour expressions name the upcoming hour; [[grammar:half negen]] therefore denotes 08:30 or 20:30 depending on context.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Hoe laat begint de film?]] asks for the starting time.
- [[grammar:De film begint om half negen.]] gives the starting time.
- [[grammar:En hoe laat eindigt de film?]] asks for the ending time.
- [[grammar:De film eindigt om elf uur.]] gives the ending time.
- [[grammar:Ik heb het kaartje.]] says that Noor has the ticket.
- [[grammar:Ik ga naar de film.]] says that Oma Anja is going to the film.
- [[grammar:Dan gaan we samen.]] links the next plan.
- [[grammar:We vertrekken om acht uur.]] gives the departure time.
- [[grammar:Het plan klopt.]] confirms the plan.
breakdown.expert:
- The two [[grammar:Hoe laat ...?]] questions use wh-first, finite-second order with subject [[grammar:de film]].
- [[grammar:om half negen]], [[grammar:om elf uur]], and [[grammar:om acht uur]] are clock-time complements.
- [[grammar:begint]] reuses Chapter 17 [[grammar:beginnen]]; [[grammar:eindigt]] introduces [[grammar:eindigen]].
- [[grammar:dan]] sequences the plan while plural [[grammar:we]] and inherited V2 remain supporting.
